message:
feat(hostcall): add ExtensionBudgetController for automatic compatibility-lane fallback

Introduce a budget controller that automatically routes extension hostcalls
to the compatibility lane when overload/anomaly signals exceed a configurable
threshold within a rolling time window, then recovers to the fast lane after
a streak of consecutive successful calls.

New types and configuration:
- ExtensionBudgetTier enum (Strict/Balanced/Throughput) with ordered presets:
  Strict(3s/2-signal/8-recovery), Balanced(8s/3-signal/16-recovery),
  Throughput(15s/5-signal/32-recovery)
- ExtensionBudgetControllerConfig with per-tier defaults and policy-mode mapping
- ExtensionBudgetFallbackState runtime tracker per extension (sliding window
  of overload timestamps, fallback flag, healthy success streak, trigger reason)

Integration into hostcall dispatch path:
- dispatch_host_call_shared: records overload signals on quota_exceeded,
  feature_extraction_budget_exceeded, and runtime_risk_decision_timeout;
  records recovery samples on every call outcome
- dispatch_shared_allowed: reactor lane overflow triggers overload signal
- hostcall_compat_kill_switch_reason: returns "forced_compat_budget_controller"
  when extension is in fallback mode

ExtensionManagerInner gains budget_controller_config and budget_fallback_states
fields with public set/get accessors and a test-only snapshot method.

Tests:
- budget_controller_tier_defaults_are_ordered: verifies strict < balanced < throughput
- budget_controller_enters_fallback_after_threshold: signal counting and fallback entry
- budget_controller_recovery_requires_consecutive_successes: streak reset on new overload
- dispatch_shared_allowed_budget_controller_forces_compat_lane: end-to-end lane routing

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
